Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Consider an event log stored in a DuckDB table named event log with the following columns case id activity timestamp A process variant is defined as the ordered sequence of activities that occur in a single case determined by sorting events within that case by timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task 1 Construct a DuckDB SQL query that determines the sequence of activities for each case id maintaining the order imposed by timestamp 2 Group cases by their complete activity sequence to identify unique process variants 3 Count how many cases correspond to each variant and determine the top K variants by their frequency 4 Return all events from the original event log that belong only to cases which are part of these top K variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The prompt does not specify what K is but it says top K variants I can either parameterize it or assume a fixed K for example 3

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will use DuckDB SQL syntax I will use string agg to create a concatenated sequence for variant identification Could also use list activity order by timestamp DuckDB supports list with order by

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then group by this list to count cases Then get top K variants by count descending Then filter events back for those case ids that have that variant list

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: I need to handle ties The prompt says top K variants by their frequency typically uses rank or row number may need to handle ties if you want exactly K variants

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: It is ambiguous Usually top K means the K variants with highest counts using count descending limit K But if there are ties for the Kth they would be excluded unless using with ties clause

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will decide to use dense rank to include ties if ranks are equal to be safe assuming that top K variants might mean the K highest frequency groups inclusive of ties

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Outline With case sequences as select case id list activity order by timestamp as variant seq from event log group by case id With variant counts as select variant seq count as case count from case sequences group by variant seq With top variants as select variant seq case count from select dense rank over order by case count desc as rank from variant counts where rank less than or equal to K

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then final select all columns from event log joined with eligible case ids where eligible case ids are obtained by joining case sequences with top variants on variant seq

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The plan correctly extracts ordered sequence per case aggregates and counts cases per variant filters down to cases belonging to top K variants and returns corresponding events from those filtered cases
